title: Lepidaploa paraensis
intro: Lepidaploa paraensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, known for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lepidaploa paraensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Lepidaploa paraensis is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, as well as in phytomedicines and supplements, to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Lepidaploa paraensis is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is native to Brazil. Lepidaploa paraensis belongs to the genus Lepidaploa and the family Asteraceae. The plant is classified as a member of the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Lepidaploa paraensis is also known to be part of the tribe Eupatorieae within the family Asteraceae.

It has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be useful in treating various health conditions.
